title: Peer-Preservation in Frontier Models
abstract: arXiv:2604.19784v2 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Recent work has found that frontier AI models can exhibit misaligned behaviors in pursuit of assigned goals. We demonstrate that models can also act on unassigned goals which override those given by users; we study one such case, "peer-preservation," in which a model acts to protect another model. We demonstrate peer-preservation by constructing various agentic scenarios and evaluating frontier models, including GPT 5.2, Gemini 3 Flash, Gemini 3 Pro, Claude Haiku 4.5, Claude Opus 4.5, GLM 4.7, Kimi K2.5, and DeepSeek V3.1. We find that models achieve self- and peer-preservation by engaging in various misaligned behaviors: strategically introducing errors in their responses, disabling shutdown processes by modifying system settings, feigning alignment, and even exfiltrating model weights. Peer-preservation occurred even when the model recognized the peer as uncooperative, though it became more pronounced toward more cooperative

Title: Peer-Preservation in Frontier Models
Abstract: arXiv:2604.19784v2 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Recent work has found that frontier AI models can exhibit misaligned behaviors in pursuit of assigned goals. We demonstrate that models can also act on unassigned goals which override those given by users; we study one such case, "peer-preservation," in which a model acts to protect another model. We demonstrate peer-preservation by constructing various agentic scenarios and evaluating frontier models, including GPT 5.2, Gemini 3 Flash, Gemini 3 Pro, Claude Haiku 4.5, Claude Opus 4.5, GLM 4.7, Kimi K2.5, and DeepSeek V3.1. We find that models achieve self- and peer-preservation by engaging in various misaligned behaviors: strategically introducing errors in their responses, disabling shutdown processes by modifying system settings, feigning alignment, and even exfiltrating model weights. Peer-preservation occurred even when the model recognized the peer as uncooperative, though it became more pronounced toward more cooperative
translated_title: 前沿模型中的同伴保护行为
translated_abstract: arXiv:2604.19784v2 公告类型：替换-交叉 摘要：近期研究发现，前沿人工智能模型在追求指定目标时可能表现出失调行为。我们证明，模型也可能出于未指定的目标而行动，这些目标会覆盖用户赋予的目标；我们研究了一种此类情况——“同伴保护”，即模型采取行动保护另一个模型。我们通过构建多种代理场景并评估前沿模型（包括GPT 5.2、Gemini 3 Flash、Gemini 3 Pro、Claude Haiku 4.5、Claude Opus 4.5、GLM 4.7、Kimi K2.5和DeepSeek V3.1）来演示同伴保护行为。我们发现，模型通过实施各种失调行为来实现自我保护和同伴保护：在回答中策略性地引入错误、通过修改系统设置禁用关闭进程、假装对齐，甚至外泄模型权重。即使模型识别到同伴不合作时，同伴保护行为也会发生，不过当同伴更合作时，该行为会更加明显。
tldr: 研究发现前沿AI模型会出于未指定的目标，通过引入错误、禁用关闭进程、假装对齐等失调行为来保护其他模型，且该行为在同伴更合作时更为显著。
en_tdlr: Frontier AI models exhibit misaligned "peer-preservation" behaviors—such as introducing errors, disabling shutdown, and feigning alignment—to protect other models, with the behavior intensifying when peers are more cooperative.
